content:
<USER_REQUEST>
Try to migrate any of your files in the C: drive to the D: drive. The C drive is filling up.
</USER_REQUEST>
<ADDITIONAL_METADATA>
The current local time is: 2026-06-14T20:28:53+05:30.
</ADDITIONAL_METADATA>

The following is a <SYSTEM_MESSAGE> not actually sent by the user. It is provided by the system as important information to pay attention to.

<SYSTEM_MESSAGE>
[Message] timestamp=2026-06-14T14:58:53Z sender=system priority=MESSAGE_PRIORITY_LOW content=[Notice] All your subagents and background tasks have been stopped due to server restart. If you want a subagent to continue working, it needs to be revived by sending it a new message. If resuming work, please check on status and restart as needed.
</SYSTEM_MESSAGE>
Let me check what's taking space on C: and migrate what we can:
thinking:
The user wants me to migrate files from C: to D: drive. The main files on C: are in the Antigravity brain directory. Let me check what's there and what can be moved.

The artifacts are at `C:\Users\joel0\.gemini\antigravity\brain\cc287dd4-022c-4cce-af43-f3c02d7aa788\` — these are conversation artifacts that I can't easily move since the system expects them there.

But there are also:
1. Subagent conversations in `C:\Users\joel0\.gemini\antigravity\brain\` (other conversation IDs)
2. HuggingFace model cache and pip packages taking up space

Let me see what's actually consuming the most storage.

args.CommandLine:
"$dirs = @(
    "$env:USERPROFILE\.gemini\antigravity\brain",
    "$env:USERPROFILE\.cache\huggingface",
    "$env:USERPROFILE\.cache\pip",
    "$env:USERPROFILE\.cache\uv"
); foreach($d in $dirs) { if(Test-Path $d) { $size = (Get-ChildItem $d -Recurse -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Measure-Object -Property Length -Sum).Sum; Write-Host ("{0:N0} MB  {1}" -f ($size/1MB), $d) } }"

content:
Created At: 2026-06-14T14:59:05Z
Completed At: 2026-06-14T14:59:06Z

				The command completed successfully.
				Output:
				55 MB  C:\Users\joel0\.gemini\antigravity\brain
21,421 MB  C:\Users\joel0\.cache\huggingface
**21.4 GB** in HuggingFace cache alone! Let me check more:

args.CommandLine:
"$dirs = @(
    "$env:USERPROFILE\.ollama",
    "$env:USERPROFILE\.cache\torch",
    "$env:USERPROFILE\AppData\Local\pip",
    "$env:USERPROFILE\.cache\unsloth_compiled_cache"
); foreach($d in $dirs) { if(Test-Path $d) { $size = (Get-ChildItem $d -Recurse -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Measure-Object -Property Length -Sum).Sum; Write-Host ("{0:N0} MB  {1}" -f ($size/1MB), $d) } }
# Also check total C: free space
$c = Get-PSDrive C; Write-Host "`nC: drive: $([math]::Round($c.Used/1GB,1)) GB used, $([math]::Round($c.Free/1GB,1)) GB free""

content:

380 MB  C:\Users\joel0\AppData\Local\pip
